skill.name: writing-plans
skill.description: Create detailed implementation plans for multi-step engineering work before code changes begin.
skill.summary: Plan complex coding work with clear tasks, files, tests, and handoff steps.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks as Ruby or shell execution and weak cryptography text matches, but SKILL.md contains planning instructions, file path examples, and sample commands only. No executable scripts, network access, credential handling, or prompt injection attempt were found, so the remaining risk is low and contextual.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Markdown Command Examples
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives. The referenced lines are Markdown code fences, inline file paths, pytest examples, and git command examples that guide plan writing; SKILL.md does not execute them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are visibly Markdown examples and inline paths, not executable skill code. There is no script file or runtime wrapper that would run these commands automatically.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Cryptography Match
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings are false positives. The cited lines describe requirements, developer assumptions, and plan goal placeholders; no cryptographic algorithm or crypto API appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text contains no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptography usage. The high severity classification appears to come from substring matching rather than semantic evidence.

content.value_statement: Complex engineering tasks often fail when planning skips file scope, tests, and handoff details. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce task-by-task plans before code changes begin.
actual_capabilities: Creates structured implementation plans for multi-step engineering tasks.
Defines exact files to create, modify, and test for each task.
Breaks work into small steps with test, implementation, verification, and commit actions.
Includes command examples and expected outcomes for verification steps.
Produces a handoff section for choosing a follow-up execution workflow.
limitations: Does not inspect code automatically unless the host assistant does that separately.
Does not execute tests, commands, or commits by itself.
Depends on the quality of the supplied requirements and repository context.
References companion skills that may not exist in every AI tool environment.

use_cases.title: Plan a feature build
use_cases.description: Turn a product requirement into small implementation tasks with files, tests, commands, and commit points.

use_cases.title: Prepare a scoped refactor
use_cases.description: Map a code cleanup into safe steps that preserve behavior and include focused regression tests.

use_cases.title: Create onboarding work instructions
use_cases.description: Write a plan that gives a new contributor enough context to implement a change safely.

prompt_templates.title: Basic feature plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you have a short requirement and need a coding plan.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an implementation plan for this feature before any code changes: [paste requirement]. Include files, tests, commands, and commit steps.

prompt_templates.title: Test-first bug fix plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when a defect needs a safe repair path.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a test-first implementation plan for this bug: [describe bug]. Include the failing test, minimal fix, verification command, and expected result.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-component delivery plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when work touches several modules or services.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan this multi-component change: [paste spec]. Break it into 2-5 minute actions with exact files, code notes, tests, and commits.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced handoff plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when another session or agent will implement the work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete handoff plan for [project change]. Assume the implementer has little codebase context. Include architecture, dependencies, task order, validation, and execution options.

output_examples.input: Plan a small API validation feature.
output: A plan file path under docs/plans with a dated feature name.
A goal, architecture summary, and technology stack section.
Tasks for writing a failing test, adding validation, running tests, and committing.

output_examples.input: Plan a bug fix for a broken import flow.
output: A focused task list that starts with a regression test.
Exact file targets for the importer, parser, and test suite.
Verification commands with expected pass or fail outcomes.

output_examples.input: Plan a refactor that must preserve behavior.
output: A staged refactor plan with behavior-preserving checkpoints.
Test coverage notes for existing and new regression cases.
A final handoff choice for implementation workflow.

best_practices: Provide the full requirement, relevant constraints, and known affected files before asking for the plan.
Ask the assistant to inspect the repository when exact paths or test commands are uncertain.
Review each planned command and commit boundary before implementation begins.
anti_patterns: Using the skill for one-line edits that do not need a formal plan.
Treating generated file paths as verified without checking the repository.
Skipping tests because the plan already describes expected behavior.

faq.question: Does this skill write code?
faq.answer: It writes implementation plans. Code changes require a separate execution step by the assistant or developer.

faq.question: Can it run tests or commands?
faq.answer: No. It can include command examples and expected results, but it does not execute them.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it before a multi-step coding task, especially when files, tests, and sequencing need clear agreement.

faq.question: Does it require repository access?
faq.answer: Repository access improves accuracy, but the skill can still draft a plan from a detailed specification.

faq.question: Is it compatible with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The metadata lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools.

faq.question: What should I review before implementation?
faq.answer: Check the file paths, test commands, task order, and assumptions before any code is changed.
